title: Training on Random Noise Levels
concept: During training, random timesteps (and their corresponding noise levels) are sampled, so the network learns to denoise at every stage of corruption.

exercises.prompt: When you look at a really blurry photo, what's hardest to do?

options.text: Guess what the clear picture originally looked like
Guess the size of the photo
Guess what color the frame is

exercises.explain: The blurrier or noisier something is, the harder it is to figure out what it originally looked like — that's exactly the challenge a denoising AI has to learn!

cards.title: Practice at Every Difficulty!
cards.body: Imagine a video game that trains you on easy, medium, AND super-hard levels — all mixed together, in random order. That's exactly how AI image generators learn to clean up noisy pictures!

cards.title: What Does This Mean?
cards.body: During training, the AI doesn't always practice on the same amount of noise. Instead, a random timestep (a random noise level) is picked each time. Sometimes the picture is barely noisy, sometimes it's almost pure static. This way, the AI learns to handle every possible stage of corruption, not just one.

cards.title: Like a Workout with Random Weights
cards.body: Picture a gym coach who makes you lift a different, random weight every single set — sometimes light, sometimes heavy. You never know which one's coming next, so your muscles get strong at ALL weight levels, not just one favorite weight.

cards.title: Mixing Up the Noise
cards.body: In one training step, the AI might see a photo with just a light sprinkle of noise (timestep 50) and practice removing a little. In the very next step, it might get a photo that's almost all static (timestep 900) and practice recovering way more. Because the timesteps are picked randomly each time, the AI becomes ready for any noise level it meets later.

cards.title: Myth: AI Trains Step-by-Step in Order
cards.body: You might think the AI practices timestep 1, then 2, then 3, in neat order like a checklist. Actually, it's the opposite — timesteps are shuffled and picked totally at random during training, so the AI doesn't get stuck expecting a predictable pattern.

cards.title: Ordered vs Random Training
cards.body: Which training style actually builds a better all-around denoiser?

left.text: Practicing noise levels 1, 2, 3... in the same order every time can make the AI only good at predictable patterns.

right.text: Practicing random noise levels each round makes the AI flexible and ready for ANY noise level, in any order, at test time.

exercises.prompt: Why does the AI train on random timesteps instead of a fixed order?

options.text: So it learns to denoise well at every possible noise level
So the images become more colorful
So the AI only needs to learn one noise level

exercises.explain: Random timesteps expose the AI to light noise, heavy noise, and everything in between, so it becomes good at denoising no matter the stage of corruption.

exercises.prompt: True or False: During training, the AI always practices removing the exact same amount of noise every time.
exercises.explain: False! The amount of noise (the timestep) is picked randomly each training round, so the AI experiences many different noise levels.

exercises.sentence: During training, ______ timesteps are sampled so the AI learns to denoise at every stage of corruption.
exercises.explain: Random timesteps are chosen so the AI gets practice at every noise level, not just one fixed stage.

exercises.prompt: A friend says: 'The AI trains on noise level 1, then always trains on noise level 2 next, in perfect order.' Is this how training actually works?

options.text: No, timesteps are picked randomly during each training step
Yes, that's exactly how it works
No, the AI never uses noise levels at all

exercises.explain: Timesteps are randomly sampled during training, not used in a fixed sequence. This randomness helps the AI generalize to all noise levels.

exercises.prompt: An AI model is being trained to generate images. Which training approach will best prepare it to denoise images at ANY stage, from barely noisy to almost pure static?

options.text: Randomly sample a different timestep (noise level) for each training example
Always train using only the maximum noise level
Always train using only the minimum noise level
Train on timesteps in a fixed increasing order every time

exercises.explain: Randomly sampling timesteps ensures the network gets balanced practice across every noise level, making it capable of denoising at any stage during real image generation.
